desc:
The grass is very short here with occasional patches of grass as high as
someone's ankles, giving protection for small animals. There are some small
fields of purple and white flowers growing between the grasses, just sticking
out their petals above the grass. To the east the rim of the forest connected to
Mirkwood can be seen, whereas in the southwest a small forest is growing.
name: Plains

desc:
There are some flowers, small plants, and weeds growing interspersed with the
very short grass. The trees begin to grow closer together as one approaches the
border of the light forest in the east, which is part of the forest west of
Mirkwood.
name: Plains near a Forest

desc:
The ground is covered by a thin layer of rotten leaves and twigs with some
freshly fallen leaves on it. Some toadstools grow on the bark and foot of the
trees in the forest. The canopy of the trees above is not fully blocking the sky
above, so some light filters through it during the day. To the west are
grasslands stretching out far to the west, to the southwest is a small
depression.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
Fallen leaves and broken off twigs make up the cover of the ground. Some small
shrubs grow here and there between the many trees, whose canopy almost blocks
out all light from the sun during daytime. To the west and south the forest gets
less dense, for the rest, the view is limited by the dense growth of trees.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
A thin layer of humus is covering the ground, being made up out of decomposed
leaves and dead plants. The canopy of the trees is so dense that during the day
barely any sunlight comes through it. Some strange species of toadstools thrive
on the humus covered ground.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The forest is composed of smaller, thinner trees to the east where the sky has
broken through the canopy. Darkness is all that is encountered to the west, as
the forest canopy closes in. A dense grassy field east of here can be seen
through the trees. Bushes push themselves between the smaller trees, competing
for the limited sunlight.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The grass is little more than three feet tall and little else can grow here. The
forests to the east and west encroach onto this field. The greater part of
Mirkwood Forest can be seen to start in the east from here. The grass has grown
wild, but would be ideal for cattle to graze upon, were it possible to lead them
here. The field continues to the south.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
The large oak trees that grow here mark the beginning of Mirkwood forest. Forest
humus litters the ground and small fungi are quick to take advantage of the
detritus. Small bushes grow here but quickly disappear to the east where the
forest becomes thick and very dark. During the day, the sun only shines through
patches in the western part of this canopy. There is little noise here other
than the breath of an occasional wind. There is a grassy field to the west and
more forest to the south.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
The sun does not exist here. The giant tree trunks in this part of Mirkwood are
all that is visible. The forest humus smells of decay, the rotten trees that
have fallen over are evidence of this. From behind every tree there seems to
emerge strange and foreboding sounds.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Large oaks and beeches grow here, forming an impenetrable canopy through which
the sun cannot be seen. The forest is enshrouded by a gloomy darkness, that is
ever present. Massive tree stumps have become the home to small animals here,
from the sounds that can be heard from this area. Whatever kind of strange
creatures that have settled here remains a mystery.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Ancient stumps covered with moss stand here. Oak trees grow scattered but seem
to cover this area with a thick canopy of branches and leaves blocking out the
sky. Little else can be seen here, other than the occasional fallen branches.
Bushes or other green flora cannot grow here, mostly because of the ever present
shadow that looms overhead.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Giant branches far overhead grow out from huge oaks in this area of the forest.
The forest floor is composed of huge rotten tree stumps, and the dark soil
exudes a strong fungal odour. Rotten mushrooms, which have grown here and are
now past their prime, add a foetid smell to the air. The smell of rot seems to
continue off to the east.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Within this part of the dark forest, strange white fungi grow. There is a foetid
odour present that hangs among the trees. The white fungi seem to be the origin
of this smell, and the piles of bone strewn among them seem to give these fungi
an ominous appearance.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Dark and gloomy trees hang overhead within a shadowed canopy. Little can be seen
here besides the gigantic tree trunks which seem to obstruct the view beyond a
few meters. Large fungi grow here out of the duff layer, which is mainly
composed of tree roots and fallen branches. A foul stench can be detected off to
the west.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The path through the forest continues on to the east through the dark and gloom
but cannot be seen very clearly. Fallen logs to the north seal off any travel in
this direction. It is dark and gloomy here, and the forest does not look
inviting. The trees seem to close in on the path to the east, which may make it
impossible to travel there.
name: A Dark Path in the Forest

desc:
The path continues to the west and to the north. It is just a small footpath
that traverses this part of Mirkwood. There are numerous fallen logs that have
not decayed yet to the east, making that part of the forest impassable. The
large trees in the south seem to grow towards this path in an attempt to disrupt
it.
name: A Dark Path in the Forest

desc:
Trees grow here in convoluted forms, spiraling up into the mists of the canopy
above. Serpentine shapes entwine one another distorting visibility here. No
further travel can be made east as the trees become impassable in that
direction. The forest continues to the west and south, while the faint traces of
a small clearing can be seen through the gloom to the north.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This area of the forest is composed mainly of the corpses of trees broken by
some huge force. Stumps of gigantic trees stand here, as well as the logs that
have fallen over. To the east, the forest becomes impassable, because of the
sheer mass of timber that has decayed and been piled there. The smell of trolls
is strong here, indicating a presence of several. The piles of bone and dung,
indicate that this may be a home to these creatures. The forest still forms a
tight canopy here, as the trees that have remained standing have grown to occupy
the area that has been disrupted.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Darkness engulfs everything here, as tall trees grow over head blocking off all
light from the sky. The forest floor is a mass of fungi, large roots and other
forest litter. Several large trees have fallen over in the east which prevents
travel in this direction. Strange sounds emerge from the forest canopy as
creatures that live in the top of the forest traverse it.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The forest looks extremely ancient here. Old decaying trees are being replaced
by larger trees, but even these are slowly being decayed by some wasting
illness. Fallen logs here are quickly being taken under and decayed by fungi.
This place reeks of decay and death. A skeleton of an animal, perhaps from some
recent kill by a carnivore lies here under one of the logs.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Dark trees grow here, twisting and bending together creating nightmarish shapes.
Several large trees grow around one very twisted shape. This large tree stands
out because of its size and its shape. Twisting around, looking very old and
hideously bent out of shape, it has large masses of stringy grey moss hanging
from it. The smell of death is strong around this centre figure of a tree.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Large trees have fallen here indicating the presence of something huge which
moved through here some time ago, as evidenced by the fungi and moss that grow
upon the logs. A disgusting odour emanates from the south, and noises of
creatures can be detected. The forest in the west becomes very dark and still.
The forest grows so thick in the east that it prevents any further travel in
that direction.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The stink of death fills the air. Mounds of bone lie piled against the dark tree
trunks of this forest. This area looks as if a slaughter took place as here are
gashes into the trunks of trees. Steaming piles of dung lie to the north,
indicating that something has moved through this area recently. There are no
sounds that would indicate any ordinary forest life here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Skylight no longer exists here, if ever it did. Large tracks of carnivorous
creatures and bones of large herbivores are present on the ground. The large
number of bones, and the stale scent of death make this place resemble a
graveyard of sorts. Perhaps it is a feeding ground of a sort.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The trees have succeeded in tenting this area in darkness. Beeches and black
oaks crowd together in this sombre forest. Dismal strands of silver-green moss
hang low from tree limbs, creating a haunted garden of broad tree trunks
emerging from layers of rotting leaves and fungi.
name: Black Shadows Forest

desc:
The dense trees branch out preventing easy travel. Barely enough light filters
through the leafy canopy to sustain the growth of forest fern here. Beech and
oak mass here in this still, sullen forest. The bark near the base of some of
the trees in this area seems to have been shredded.
name: Sombre Trees

desc:
The darkness of this oak forest is enhanced by the growing number of black alder
here. Shadows loom deeply, allowing only glimpses inside this tight mass of tree
trunks and wispy trails of almost glowing, pale-green mosses and not much else.
The deep layer of decay and mulch that cushions this black forest floor prevents
noise from escaping.
name: Wailing Woods

desc:
Plains continue here within these mighty forest walls. Travel is possible north
or south as fields can be seen in these directions. The trees lining this area
grow extremely dense and shut out light within their woody depths.
name: The Narrows

desc:
The haunting and dark forest surrounds you in all directions except to the
south. To the north the trees are less crowded than east or west where it is
impossible to navigate. This forest plain is dotted with downed tree trunks,
bracken and forest foliage. Not much light of day illuminates this field as the
tall shadows of the surrounding forest prevent it.
name: Woods Hole

desc:
The forest of darkness is bathed in a humid silence. Strong limbs and boughs of
these strong trees hold up the leafy tent, preventing much light of any kind
from entering. Moisture seeping up from the forest floor has created a boggy
depression between these trees that almost prevents passage to the north. Tall
beeches and oaks occupy most of the acreage here.
name: Mirky Shadows

desc:
Darkness dwells here, as light is not common in the deep reaches of Mirkwood
Forest. Broad tree trunks barricade the entry east as the woods take every
foothold in that direction. The stillness and humidity in this part of the
forest is strongly apparent. Strange sticky webbings of some kind connect many
of the tree limbs.
name: Surrounding Forest

desc:
Stillness hushes these woods into an unnatural silence. Wisps of haunting
green-grey mosses hang down from forest limbs. The one constant within this
forest with its mature trees and dense canopy is the humidity. A strange humid
heaviness fills this forest.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The forest intensifies both in the number of mature trees and in the looming
darkness. These trees compete with each other for sky views by stretching limbs
ever wider and higher and ultimately preventing any light from ever reaching the
forest floor. Long hauntingly pale whisps of moss drape down from the branches
above. The stands of mature trees block any passage to the east.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Whispers of light drizzle down, creating long shadows between the old timbers.
Mosses grow up the north sides of the trunks here. To the east the trees have
grown so densely as to seem almost fused at their trunks, making travel east
impossible. The layers of leaves and other forest debris that cover the forest
floor encourage fungi growth around the exposed tree roots.
name: Shadows Way

desc:
The thick forest walls create a silent surrounding in all directions. The sullen
shadows of these ancient trees hide in the darkness of the forest floor deep
with countless layers of rotten leaves. Humidity hangs thickly in the air. Rough
tree bark is softened by mosses creeping up the northern faces of the trunks.
name: Forest of Shadows

desc:
The trees here have such a thick canopy that very little sunlight penetrates
through. It is difficult to tell whether it is day or night. The forest floor is
quite roomy except for the space the trunks take up. Except for these trees,
nothing seems to grow on the ground, due to the lack of daylight.
name: Treetop Canopy

desc:
Darkness swallows all the moonlight and sunlight that sneaks into this black
grove of oaks. Gnarled oaks link limbs forming an almost watertight umbrella
preventing any measure of rain from reaching the forest floor. The conditions
here may attribute to the stillness that hangs thick in this grove.
name: Forest Tomb

desc:
The dense grove of beech and oak tightens with branches and leaves covering the
sky, preventing any light from intruding here. Glossy green-grey leaves and
white berries drape from bare tree limbs as if they belong there. Old toppled
logs and layers of rotting leaves carpet the forest floor.
name: Shade of Mirkwood

desc:
Oppressive silence hangs here like the moss from the tree branches. Knotty tree
roots emerge from the forest humus. Large coral shaped fungi poke out between
the emergent roots, taking in the available moisture and nutrients. Some of
these fungi are very large, having grown old in this darkness.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Darkness consumes all light here, as trees of unimagined size curl and twist in
all kinds of cruel shapes. Roots of all kinds bend and twirl through the forest
floor. A gloomy oppressive silence hangs here, amid the forest canopy. Through
the branches to the north a very pale light can be seen.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The trees here grow in ordered rows heading to the north and east, except for
one in particular tree. Most of the trees that grow here tangle with other
trees, forming a matted canopy of mostly thick boughs and branches. There is a
lack of a rotting smell which is common throughout most of the other forest
except for the peculiar tree, which has small bones piled against its trunk. A
strange eldritch light seems to appear faintly off to the northwest.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The twisted trees that grow here do so in what could be described as rows. It
looks as if these trees were planted in a special arrangement. Their boughs and
branches intertwine and form a tight canopy. Gone are the smells typical of the
forest. Gone are also the typical forest organisms of decay, such as fungi. A
strange pale light can be detected off to the west.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
There is a swath of destruction running east to west. Large animal bones lie
here scattered among those trees still standing. Stumps and uprooted trees lay
strewn about the makeshift path of some large creature. There is a foul stench
in the air of trolls, mingled with the smell of death. There is a deathly silent
here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Oaks and beeches grow in distorted, almost nightmarish shapes here. Trunks of
some of the trees stretch into one another merging to become one gigantic tree
all the while rising into the darkened canopy above. The forest humus reeks of
fungi, as mushrooms abound here, living off the decay and nutrients of the
detritus.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The path that meanders into the east and west from here is not well maintained
as the forest has severely encroached upon it, covering it in places with large
roots from the trees to the north and south. At times, rustling can be heard in
the forest canopy above, as small forest creatures travel along.
name: A Dark Path

desc:
This part of the trail is particularly dark and gloomy, and the canopy overhead
is seemingly endless. Large bulbous fungi grow on the sides of the path. Few
weeds grow here, choked off by the ever present forest. The path continues to
the east as well as leading off to the west. The forest grows to the south and
in the north, a strong foetid odour emanates.
name: A Dark Path in the Forest

desc:
Beech trees collide with oaks here as one glade overlaps another. Creeks and
groans can be heard as the larger oaks rise above into a dark canopy. Huge
branches can be seen intermingling between large oaks and beeches. The forest
floor is covered with thin papery bark of the beeches and pale white mushrooms
abound. A small trail can be seen to the north through the darkness.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The forest looks partially destroyed as something large has been moving to the
east and to the west. The ground which is normally composed of mushrooms and
rotting logs is smashed and upturned. Large tracks can be seen on the ground.
The stink of decay pervades this area and grows ever worst to the east. Only
darkness can be seen in all directions.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This area of the forest is devoid of decay. These trees here grow in rows which
seem to head in a southerly direction from here. The silence here is one of
anticipation, as if the forest is holding its breath. Gone are the sounds that
are common in other parts of the forest. The canopy overhead is composed of
branches that grow tightly together. There is a strange pale light to the south.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Silence and darkness engulf this part of the forest. The trees seem to grow in
ordered rows and show no signs of normal decay. Huge branches from each tree
grow together in the canopy overhead making a thatched-like roof. A pale light
can be seen to the north far off.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Smells of rot and decay assail the senses. Fungi of all types grow here between
the large tree roots which also poke themselves out of the ground. Massive tree
trunks grow here, stretching up into the blackness above. No sounds can be heard
around here, and this makes the silence of this part of the forest oppressive.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Neither the Sun, nor the Moon can easily find routes to give light to the dark
forest floor. All the plant life that covers the ground seems dead or rotting,
except for the fungi which aid in decomposition. Light green moss hangs from the
tree limbs like decrepit bandages.
name: Deep Forest Darkness

desc:
The mature tree trunks in this part of the forest are as wide as two men. Deep
humus littering the forest floor absorb sounds and with noticeable effect. Leafy
vines with clinging discs creep up the tree trunks towards the sky. Several of
the trees here have deep scars shredded into the bark.
name: Oak Grove

desc:
Long trails of green-grey mosses hang down from the leafy ceiling, creating a
ghostly effect. The silence of the forest can be attributed to the thick spongy
layers of spent vegetation cushioning the forest floor.
name: Still Trees

desc:
Thick gnarled trunks of mature trees crowd this forest, embracing only still
darkness. The ceiling is packed tight with the foliage of these old trees.
Humongous roots reach up in tangled masses through the rot of the forest floor
making passage to the south impossible. A few of the massive trunks have been
ravaged by fire leaving only blackened charred hollows behind.
name: Tangled Trees

desc:
The different grasses in this meadow provide interesting hues of green and gold
allowing views of subtle beauty in all directions. A few trees dot the softly
rolling landscape, growing in greater numbers to the east and preventing travel
to the north.
name: Vale Meadow

desc:
The darkness of this forest is enhanced by the growing number of mature alder
here. Shadows loom deep, allowing only glimpses inside this tight mass of
tree-trunks and wispy trails of almost glowing, pale-green mosses and not much
else. The thick layer of decay and mulch that cushions this dark forest floor
prevents any noise from escaping. The immense trunks of these trees prevent
passage to the west.
name: Alders

desc:
The forest suddenly slopes somewhat upwards. It is not a steep slope but the
incline seems fairly long and ongoing. Maybe it feels longer due to the severe
density of the woods making it hard to walk with any speed here.
name: Woody Slope

desc:
There is a great mix of different trees here. Beeches, oaks and alder trees join
the pines that dominate these woods. For some reason this part of the forest
seems inhabited only by very young trees. There are no mature trees at all to be
found here.
name: Young Forest

desc:
The edge of the forest curves widely here. The plains extend to the west and
south, but the severely dense forested area prevents any access to these plains.
Pine trees are the most dominant trees in this area along with black oaks.
name: Forest Bend

desc:
Strange trees with wide green leaves make the forest here very crowded. On the
ground there is a thick layer of spent foliage. Travelling through these woods
can be as difficult as travelling through thick snowdrifts. To the west the
woody debris has piled high making travelling in that direction tiresome.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The trees in this area have very short trunks so they resemble gigantic bushes.
These stunted trees make it difficult for a grown man to walk upright without
hitting the lower branches. To the north and east extremely dense vegetation
prevents access to the forest.
name: Forest Corner

desc:
New young saplings of black oak, beech and black alder crowd this forest
entrance with bright supple greenery. The soil is enriched with a dark brown
mulch of rotting vegetation. Though many varieties of ferns can be found here,
the dominant mother fern is especially abundant.
name: Saplings

desc:
Stands of mature oak dominate this sparsely forested area. The forest floor is
soft to the foot, covered in the mulch of many seasons debris. Sword fern sprout
in bright green clumps amid the tall trunks and shadows.
name: Stands of Oak

desc:
Mature beech and black oak protect the forest floor from the glare of the sun
and the curiosity of the moon. Only sharp slivers of light make it to the bottom
of the forest, and then only when conditions are right. Drifts of forest refuse
rots back into soil with the passing seasons.
name: Shadowy Woods

desc:
Beech trees reach their long limbs toward the sky, dappling this light forest in
the day and shrouding it by night. Ivy grows rampant along the forest floor and
up the trunks of these trees. The forest grows darker in all directions but
west.
name: Dappled Darkness

desc:
Darkness pervades this area, where the trees grow so tall and thick as to block
out any and all sky. Thick branches grow together above, while large roots poke
out of the ground below which makes walking tricky at best. Trees seem to
continue in all directions.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Visibility is obscured here through the large trees. Looking past one massive
trunk, all that can be seen is another tree. The trees grow in a chaotic mass of
density, twirling and entwining one another sometimes more like vines than oaks.
The stench of rot and death are prevalent here. Quiet rustling in the canopy
above is from small creatures, as chirps and whistles can be heard. The forest
seems to extend in all directions.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The forest floor here has not seen sunlight for ages. Small white fungi of
distorted shapes bend and twist, covering the humus. Huge roots from the dark
gigantic trees jut out of the ground making this area very bumpy. The small
areas make ideal holes for the small forest creatures that inhabit this forest.
The forest becomes ominously dark to the east, but seems to lighten to the west.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Colossal trees are swallowed up into the darkness above as they grow together to
form a dense canopy of leaves and branches. Shrill whistles and chirps of
woodland creatures that are common to Mirkwood can be heard all around. The
smell of rot permeates the air here, as fallen logs are disintegrated by large
fungi that grow here. The air is damp, and the ground moist which are perfect
conditions for the fungi here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The trees here grow in a strange semblance of rows. There is a lack of bones
fungi or other things associated with the forest in other parts of the wood.
Where the forest is quiet in other places, this area is deathly still. To the
southeast a strange eldritch light can be seen.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Large trees look as if they have been pushed aside here and uprooted, as some
creature or group of creatures have moved through here. The forest continues on
all sides. The smell of rot and decay is strong here, and continue to the east.
Large tracks lead off to the east and to the west.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This part of the dark forest contains a large concentration of beeches. The
abnormally large white trees give the appearance of standing in a giants rib-
cage. The forest canopy seems lighter in places, however no sky can be seen.
Large white mushrooms grow here between the beeches, providing sharp contrast to
the surrounding darkness. There is a sickly sweet aroma to this area, mixed in
with the smell of forest rot.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This trail is nothing more than a small dirt path. To the north and south the
forest grows closely together. The trail here is exceedingly dusty and each step
produces more clouds of dust, which adds to the lack of visibility. The smell of
rot is strong along this section of the trail, coming from both sides of the
forest. The path continues along heading both east and west.
name: A Darkening Path

desc:
The path becomes increasingly dark both here and into the east. It lightens up
and broadens somewhat to the south. Dark trees from the forest crowd the trail
reducing it to nothing more than a small footpath. Travel is difficult at best.
There is no sky visible, as the canopy grows closely together.
name: A Darkening Path

desc:
The large dark trees of the forest seem to grow together in an attempt to block
out this offending trail with some success. In places the trail becomes nothing
more than a small line in between huge trunks. Strange chirps and rustlings of
small forest creatures can be heard from the canopy above which lets in little
sunlight. The trail becomes ever darker as it leads off in the north, while it
also seems to disintegrate into the darkness to the west.
name: Trail in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
Massive oaks grow here in a twisted manner, looking as though they had been
tortured rather than grown naturally. Some trees have huge gashes in their
sides, from claw marks of some unnatural beast that had passed here. Several
trees have fallen over, and mushrooms and even the humus have been upturned by
the passing of something large heading east. A trail of sorts can be seen to the
north, while the forest continues to darken to the south.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Large trees grow here, strangling out the skylight with their mighty branches
that tangle themselves together to form a ceiling of sorts. There are no large
fungi or forest creatures which can normally be associated with this forest.
Another strange thing is the lack of bones or smell of rot, except for a single
humanoid skeleton.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Trees of considerable size crowd out all skylight here. However one large branch
hangs low enough to be reached. The tree roots here jut out from every corner of
the humus creating tangles everywhere. Small mushrooms grow on every root
feeding of the rich nutrients of the wood and surrounding humus. The stench of
wood rot hangs in the air.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Massive oaks grow here in a tangled mass of tree after tree. Branches grow
together in a mass of wood that chokes out all light from above. Roots from
underground poke out tangling much that grows here. Rot and decay are seen here
as there are many different bones and skulls strewn about, particularly near the
larger of the roots growing here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This is the outskirts of the large forest to the east of here. Trees here grow
tall enough to block out most of the sky, though in patches, sunlight can pierce
through the darkness. The smell of rot and decay is evident here, and seems to
continue off to the east. To the west a large grassy field can be seen. On the
forest ground, there are tracks of small forest creatures coming to and from
this area. Faint rustlings all around can be heard as these small creatures move
to and fro.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
The huge trees here become ever larger as they continue to grow eastwards
merging into an increasingly darker canopy. Huge clumps of greenish-grey moss
hang from the branches of these trees, and large bracket fungi grow from the
trunks. These trees look like they have been here for a very long time. Huge
tree roots extend from the ground, giving the forest floor a rough and bumpy
appearance.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
Tall majestic oak trees grow here blocking out most of the sun. Roots from these
trees sprout from the ground intertwining themselves with one another making the
soil uneven and travel difficult. Dark shelf fungi growing from the trunks seem
to be one of the only things that can grow in this darkness. The forest
continues to the north as well as to the south of here. To the east the tree
trunks become darker and taller still. It seems lighter to the west.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The lofty trees here do not grow close enough to prevent the sun from shining
through the tops. The forest floor is littered with the leaves of seasons past
rotting timber and many varieties of fern. Leafy vines attach themselves to the
tree trunks, in pursuit of daylight.
name: Trees in the Forest

desc:
This sheltered meadow is protected by the forest in all directions but south.
This location makes for longer nights with the long shadows of the forest close
by. The northern facing tree trunks are awash with bright green moss. Shady
forest flowers and ferns abound.
name: Forest Frame

desc:
The mature forest is close to the east across this meadow. Thorny thistle and
stinging nettles hide within the wheat fields covering this area. Small flowers
top stout stems dotting these grasses with texture and colour. The bothersome
weeds can make passage by foot painful and slow-going.
name: Forest Border

desc:
The broad mixture of mature trees diffuses the sky's offerings. Scrubby
groundcover grows waist-high in some areas. To the south the woods open up to
golden pastures.
name: Forest Edges

desc:
The trees give way to an open expanse of ferns and woody rhododendrons. Wild
thorny hedges ramble into the glen to the west, beyond a well-tended garden.
Soft mosses blanket the ground beneath the rich variety of forest vegetation.
name: Woods Glen

desc:
Fescue and other grasses grow shorter here, and wide stony patches of light
brown earth are dotted with small shrubs and bushes. To the east the plains
filter out and a loose glade of ferns thrives near the gloomy forest wall.
name: West of the Forest

desc:
The dark forest to the east stretches to the north and south, thick as a castle
wall, with few negotiable entryways. Dainty monkshood, velvety ground mosses and
forest ferns grow here nourished by the moisture of daily dew and protected by
the heavy shadows of the forest. Decaying logs lay haphazardly throughout these
meadows, making travel cumbersome.
name: Plains along the Forest

desc:
The meadows meet the forest here with fallen trees and an array of ferns and
forest flowers. Thin white stems topped by round white seed heads colour the
green, looking like tiny magical wands. Clumps of dead foliage and leaves have
accumulated in this sheltered meadow creating a natural haven for the wildlife
here. There is no entryway into the forest to the east, it is too thick with
trees.
name: Against the Forest Wall

desc:
The wild plains have fanned out here to form a tame plush carpet of seemingly
uniform blue-green fescue. Slight depressions and gentle rolling meadows are
softened by the thick cushioning of this luxuriant lawn. There is no shelter in
this open sweep of green.
name: Fairway Turf

desc:
Wildflowers and flowering shrubs colour these meadows all year round. Hard red
fruit speckle the woody stemmed, evergreen brambles that grow freely. Beautiful
blooms including tulip, crocus, anemone and lilies are strongly represented with
their colourful display. A few crab-apple trees dot these fields.
name: Flowered Meadows

desc:
Grasses including wheat, oat and fescue prosper in these fields. To the north
tangled masses of prickly brambles prevent travel in that direction. Off to the
far west not much can be seen aside from the Misty Mountains. The entrance to a
forest is east of here.
name: Open Fields

desc:
Thorns and prickly leaves cover many bushes and broad vines. Many varieties of
berries thrive in the rich soil provided by forest. There are short bushes with
leathery dark green leaves and ground-hugging evergreen heathers. West of here a
tall hedge does not allow travel in that direction.
name: Sweet Berry Patch

desc:
Long crowded rows of mature trees form a wide grassy alley. It stretches east-
west and grows too densely to allow penetration through the trees to the north.
The grasses growing here are of various species. The ryegrass and fescue are
most dominant of the grasses and spurge is also quite plentiful.
name: Alley of Trees

desc:
The forest here is fairly open and the most common tree here seems to be oak.
Hardy vines grow across the forest floor and up the large trunks. This finger of
the forest is interspersed with small forest meadows and young saplings. To the
south the trees grow too densely to travel in that direction.
name: Meadow Trees

desc:
The dry earth is swathed in patches of sick-yellowed grasses. The many stones
exposed above the ground look to be as promising a crop as possible here. The
poor soil and the lack of water prevents further vegetation here. The shadows of
the forest close in on the edges of this field.
name: Eerie Meadow

desc:
This field is formed of small bunch-grasses. To the east the grasses become
intermingled with large bushes and further east, large dark trees can be seen.
The sandy soil that these grasses sprout from is very dry and to the south
becomes drier still. A small glade of trees can be seen to the west where
moisture probably abounds.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
This grass field continues to the north and south. The sandy soil prevents much
else from growing here besides the short bunch grasses. A few small boulders
have come to rest here, with blue and white lichens growing on them. To the west
a border of very large trees can be seen. The grass grows to this border and
then stops forming a line between this field and the forest trees. To the west
the grasses meet up with sedges and a few large boulders can be seen off in that
direction.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
This field is surrounded by large trees to the north and east. These trees seem
to be the start of some immense forest where light seldom penetrates. The
grasses here meet these trees and stop at the forest border, not daring to grow
further. A field to the west seems to have been abandoned a long time ago. The
grass field here stretches into the south.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
Small saplings of beeches meet larger oaks to the east as the beginnings of
Mirkwood forest takes shape. Bushes and grasses that grow here quickly disappear
into the encroaching forest. A few large vines have taken up residence in this
part of the forest. The canopy quickly becomes dark to the east, but here, the
sun is able to pour through.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
This area of the forest looks as if it were damaged by a fire a long time ago.
Many of the larger trees look scorched by fire. Some of them have even been
hollowed out, making them home for small creatures. The darkness that is so
common in the other parts of the forest is just as prevalent here. The forest
continues in all directions.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Gloomy darkness is all that is encountered here. Strange dark trees grow here
blocking out all light and sky. The lowest branches of the trees are covered by
strange looking stringy grey moss. The forest floor is home to several varieties
of mushrooms. There is a stench of decay here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Gigantic branches, barren of leaves, reach down from the darkness of the canopy.
Huge trunks, many laden with shelf fungi, grow scattered here. The forest floor
is uneven at best with many roots having grown through the humus tearing it up.
Strange pale plants have sprouted up here, alongside these large roots. The
smell of decay is strong here, and it seems to emanate from the east and west.
To the north, a small trail can barely be discerned through the gloom.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
This dusty trail seems seldom maintained given that many roots from the forest
have found their way onto the path. So many gnarled roots make the road hard to
follow in spots. The only way for one to realise this is even a road at times is
to spot the clear areas between the trees. Large mushrooms grow on the sides of
this trail. Through the gloomy darkness the trail can be seen to the east and
west, while the forest trees tower above to the north and south.
name: Trail in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
Massive trees grow here, distorted in shapes unimaginable. Trees and branches
twist and mesh together which completely obstruct any sign of skylight. Roots of
massive sizes grow out of these large hideous looking trees. Several piles of
bones are near some of the larger roots. A trail seems visible to the south,
while a smaller path is visible to the east.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The trees become ever taller, disappearing into the darkness above. If a canopy
exists it cannot be seen from here. Death and rot are all that exist on the
forest floor. Large trees which have fallen over, resemble corpses with fungi
growing over them. The occasional skeleton of a large herbivore lies here gazing
absently into space with its hollow eye sockets.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Stories are told of how dark and dreary this trail is, but they do it no
justice. Hardly any light shines in during the day making the forest canopy
overhead very oppressive. Strange noises can be heard from every corner of the
wood. In some places this trail is little more than a footpath, overgrown with
large roots jutting out of the ground.
name: Trail in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
A distinct smell of decaying things taints this part of the forest. Skeletal
remains of many animals and a few humanoid creatures lie strewn about the forest
floor. Rot pervades this area as small, oddly shaped fungi decay fallen giant
logs into the humus. Strange rustling sounds can be heard from all around, but
no source can be determined. If there is a canopy it cannot be seen, as the
darkness above reveals nothing. The darkness and smell of rot continue into the
east. A faint clearing of what may be a path can be seen to the north.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Large forbidding trees offer no sign of light from above. There is no noise from
the usual forest creatures. The only life that lives here in the darkness
besides the trees themselves are small fleshy fungi that make use of the forest
litter.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
Huge towering oaks block out all light here. Menacing trees with huge branches
grow here, giving this forest a dreadful appearance. Gone are the sights and
sounds normally associated with forest life. This dark forest continues to the
east and north. Through the dark haze present in this forest to the south and
west grass fields can be seen.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
The grassy plain that exists here is overshadowed by large trees in the east and
north, where an immense forest seems to begin. South of this field lies the
remnants of a cultivated hay field. West of here the short grass from this field
continue.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
An old hay field exists here. At one time this field was cultivated for hay or
alfalfa, or some grass. The reasons for this field's existence have long since
disappeared as has most of its cultivated appearance. Old fence posts dot the
borders of this field, in a feeble effort it seems to keep out the dense grassy
fields that surround it.
name: High Hay

desc:
Short bunch grasses abound here, not allowing growth for anything else. The only
things that prevent this field from being flat are large rocks that jut out from
the centre. The rocks seem somewhat out of place, but do not yield to the
growing grasses that surround them. Lichens have taken residence upon these
rocks, giving them a slightly bluish tinge. An old wheat field lies north of
here while grassy fields can be seen to the east and west. A glade of small
trees lies to the south.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
Small trees of oak and beech make up this glade, providing shade for a small
pond in the centre of these woods. Tracks of small animals can be seen going to
and from this waterhole. Small grasses abound here as these trees only provide
shade and do not choke out the sky as the nearby forests seem to. Ferns and
other fragile plants grow close to the pond, depending on it for much needed
moisture.
name: In a Forest Glade

desc:
The forest here is mainly immature midsize trees with narrow trunks that are
branchless until they form the crown of the tree. The treetops are widespread
and form a roof which allows just a little sunlight to filter to the ground,
even though the trees are not too close to each other. To the east there is an
opening in the forest forming a wide river of grass.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Mature oaks and beech trees grow together in this quite open and sunny part of
the forest. Several broad ancient trees with branches and boughs full of leaves
stand above the rest. Young fiddlehead bracken prosper here in the seasons of
dead leaves and vegetation strewn about the forest floor. To the west and south
plains open up and the forest ends.
name: Mixed Grove

desc:
Small rambling bushes and thick green grass make up most of the terrain here. A
few withered oaks reach out with scraggly limbs. Not far to the north, the edges
of the forest can be seen.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The green landscape is covered by a thick sod of ryegrass and fescue which is
about the height of a man's boot. It slopes gently to the west where a small
grove of peonies thrives. To the east a tall wide-stretched hedge grows wild and
hinders access to what lies beyond.
name: Plains by a Hedge

desc:
Brambles, stickers and sharp nettles have overtaken the plains grasses. Travel
here is difficult even by mount. Underfoot, the rock-hewn soil is matted with
prickly weeds that catch and snag everything passing through.
name: Scrub Weeds

desc:
Clumping ryegrass covers this plains. The ryegrass has choked out any other
plants here, with the exception of a small grove of dwarf holly. To the west,
the plains drop off into the depth of a ravine, making travel in that direction
impossible.
name: Ryegrass Pastures

desc:
The green grass is accompanied by evergreen shrubs and wild roses. The colours
of these sweetly scented roses make this meadow as spectacular as any here in
this vale. The scene can be dazzling with a bright blue sky behind white billowy
clouds. A steep ravine to the west prevents travel in that direction.
name: Rose Meadow

desc:
The grassy plains drop off to the east as a steep ravine has left a deep cut in
this lush vale. A few scrub oaks peer over the edges of the drop. This ravine
can be seen running north to south - then cutting east to west. These fields lie
at its junction. The severe cut of this ravine makes travel east or south
unthinkable.
name: Steep Ravine

desc:
A deep ravine cuts through the fields to the east. The meadows are mostly weeds
and grasses. A few dead trees can be seen lifting barren branches towards the
sky. Lupine are scattered throughout the plains, adding ribbons of colour.
Travel to the east is impossible as the north-south edges of the steep ravine
cannot be traversed.
name: Ravine on the Green

desc:
Weeds compete with rocks covering the ground. Sharp prickly stems covered with
barbed seed heads await passers-by. An ancient pine tree stands royally amid the
fields, seeming to rule the countryside with its presence. Small pinecones lie
scattered like gifts beneath its boughs. Fields continue unbroken in every
direction.
name: A Lone Pine

desc:
The earth is more compacted here and does not allow the vegetation to reach its
peak. Small shrubbery and tough ground-cover only allow tight clumps and mounds
of grass to share space in the topsoil. To the north the richer green line of
foliage implicates a water source.
name: Fallowed Grounds

desc:
A shimmering pool of fresh water fed by an underground spring forms a small
mere. The edges are quite shallow allowing animals to come here to drink the
clear and cool water. A few scattered leaves float on the surface of the pond.
There is a small, faint path around the pool that leads south.
name: Small Mere

desc:
Various common shrubs, weeds and small vines claim much of these tall pastures.
Pesky brambles have flourished here and are covered with tiny green leaves and
sharp brown thorns. The odd scent of this particular plant offers a musky
scented undertone to the sweet floral perfume of the many wildflowers growing
here. There are clusters of fennel clumps sprouting up in irregular patterns.
name: Rambling Brambles

desc:
The plains give way to a wide glen of ferns, including pointy-edged sword ferns.
A multicoloured froth of heath cushions the footing below. Short tufts of fescue
are interspersed with outcroppings of stone. Through a fissure in the rock, a
tiny trickle of water saturates the ground nourishing the subtle array of fern,
fungi and lichen.
name: Fern Haven

desc:
Dappled sunlight pierces the shade of the beech trees, nurturing forest lilies
and various fungi grow along the floor of this area. Despite the sunlight there
is a gloom about the entire grove. One may notice the lack of any bird song.
Through the trees, to the west and south there are open areas.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
Small trees sparsely populate this forest, letting the sun pour in. This allows
small bushes to grow here as well as certain mushrooms. Oaks and small beeches
grow here in the moist ground. Ferns grow in abundance here and continue to grow
off in the west, where they can be seen in a green waves of vegetation. To the
south and east, this forest continues. Grassy fields can be seen to the north.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
Tall grasses mingle with sedges that grow here. To the south a few trees sprout
up, while the forest to the west is dense and dark. The grasses that grow here
provide perfect cover for small animals, evidenced by the rustlings, creaks and
chirps which can be heard all around. The northern grass field seems composed of
just one kind of grass.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
This field is covered with small mounds that serve as havens to rodents. Gophers
abound in this field of bunch-grasses, darting into and out of their tunnels.
The grass serves as the perfect cover for these animals. The grasses meet
forested land to the west. To the east there seems to be an old abandoned hay
field. These bunch-grasses continue to the north and south. The fields to the
west end at several fallen logs, and from there the forest takes over.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
There are two varieties of grass that grow here. From the north a taller variety
meets a shorter one coming from the south and east. These grasses compete for
space in this field. The grasses meet brush and forest in the west.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
The tall grasses from the north seem to continue to grow here. Five feet in
height, this grass seems to continue for a ways to the south. Large trees grow
in the east, indicating the start of a large forest. More forested land can be
seen to the west as well.
name: Tall Grasses

desc:
The grass field here is taller than its surroundings. The ground is moist here
in spots perhaps indicating the presence of an underground spring. The grasses
that grow here have taken advantage of this, reaching heights of more than five
feet. To the east and north tall trees can be seen in the distance.
name: Tall Grasses

desc:
Several kinds of broadleaf trees, as well as some rather dark unrecognizable
varieties grow here, blocking out the sun. Subtle rustlings of small dark
creatures scurrying through the canopy are all that can be heard here. A trail
to the north, as well as the fields to the west and south, look more open.
Darkness is all that can be seen to the east as the forest closes in.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
This is the entrance to Mirkwood Forest. The trees are so thick that they almost
form walls, hindering anything in going north or south while encouraging travel
along the trail. The forest canopy encroaches from the surrounding forest
choking off most of the sky. A rusted gate stands here, remnant of a time long
past. A small spring trickles water which forms a pool off to the north side of
the trail, giving travellers a chance to drink before heading off in either
direction. To the west the trail cuts through open field, while to the east the
trail dissolves into the darkness of this great forest.
name: The Gate to Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Large trees grow here and sunlight disappears the further east this dark forest
continues. Grassy fields lie west of here and seem more inviting than this
place. Darkness engulfs this area in spots and strange noises abound. A rotten
stench can be detected and grows stronger towards the east. A clearing can be
seen to the south where a trail seems to meet the forest.
name: A Foreboding Forest

desc:
This large grassy field extends north and south, where in the distance a trail
can be seen running through it. Short bunch-grasses grow everywhere stopping
only when they reach the eastern and western borders, where the field meets the
forest. No bushes grow here, unable to compete with the sheer mass of the grass.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
This three foot wide trail cuts through the dense grasses growing here and to
the north and south. The trail cuts west into dark forest, and east into the
even darker forest of Mirkwood. There is little sign of foot traffic here,
although the trail is somewhat maintained. The open sky can be seen here, quite
in contrast to the oppressive dark canopies seen in the eastern and western
forests.
name: A Trail Leading to the Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The bunch-grasses growing from the north and south are densely packed here.
There is little sign of anything else growing, although a small shrub or flower
makes an occasional appearance, adding to the green colour, which dominates this
field. A dark forest appears at the western border and the Mirkwood Forest
begins in the east.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
Grasses grow in bunches here, packed in tightly as they compete for space. There
are a few small bushes off to the west where in the distance a forest can be
seen. The grasses are uniformly three feet in height and this field continues to
the east for some miles. To the south, a small grove of trees is visible.
name: Dense Grass

desc:
This forested area is composed mostly of beeches and small leafy bushes. The
ground is soft and covered in old fallen leaves. Small mushrooms poke through
this thin layer of leaves. There are grassy fields in the east and north as the
forest opens up there. To the west the forest becomes dark and still.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
Small beeches grow here with small bushes providing ample view of the sky.
Grasses can be seen to the east, while the forest grows more thick to the south
and west. To the north the forest continues to open up. Several rose bushes grow
wild here.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
The forest air is thick and stifling. The trees are very large here and block
out the sky above. The forest here is dark and provides excellent conditions for
the mushrooms that grow here. Fallen trees to the east make access that way
impossible. The forest seems to continue to the north, west and south.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The oak trees that grow here seems to have been here for a long time, as large
shelf fungi grow on the trunks. Large mushrooms cover the forest floor
benefiting from the ample shade these trees provide. Masses of vegetation and
fallen timber hamper travel to the east but it is still possible. The forest
continues to the north and west. A mass of green ferns can be seen to the south
as the forest opens to a small glade. The forest empties onto a small field in
the east.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The forests that surround you have allowed for a small glade of open ground to
mysteriously appear. The ground here is very moist and small puddles appear
frequently. Large fronds seem to sprout everywhere as ferns have taken up
residence here. It is so humid here fungi can be seen dotting the ground
everywhere. The ferns seem to continue to the south into a protected alcove.
name: Glade of Ferns

desc:
A loose piling of toppled trees forms a protected alcove; perfect conditions for
this beautiful fern glade. Hardy chain fern, lacy maidenhair fern and spiked
sword fern flourish here creating an almost faerie-like wonderland. Soft mats of
multi-hued mosses, nourished by the early dew, colour the rich earth in velvety
softness. Looking east the shadowy despair of the dark forest looms.
name: Fern Alcove

desc:
A massive blue rock juts up like a fortress through the ground here. Centuries
have weathered the rock smooth leaving its face quite steep. Seepage from above
has produced a verdant blanket of moss. There are lots of small holes burrowed
in the ground here. All around this natural monument the grass is low and it
almost looks as if it has been uniformly trimmed short.
name: Mossy Rock

desc:
Towering spikes of hollyhocks and colourful mallow add height and interest to
the grassy plains. Purple and green clover and ground-hugging creepers grow
rampantly under the tallest layer of vegetation. The topsoil is dotted with
small stones and pebbles.
name: Hollyhocks

desc:
The mature grasses here are particularly high and quite dense. Animals as large
as deer can hide easily in this deep vegetation. The microclimate here and the
richness of the soils in this pasture keep it lush. Travel through this area can
be slow, as it is on a steep grade.
name: Mature Pastures

desc:
Gentle hills amid these plains cause a watercolour landscape of lush green
vegetation mixed with golden ripe grasses atop the rises. Weeds always seem to
be ready with seed heads and are certainly plentiful here.
name: Mixed Grasses

desc:
Grasses as high as a tall man's waist carpet these plains. The plains to the
west are broken by the swirling blue waters of the Anduin. Beyond the river, the
peaks of the Misty Mountains brush up against the western skies.
name: Green Grassy Fields

desc:
Uncommonly high grasses grow throughout this broad grove of crab apple trees, as
the ground dips uncomfortably towards the centre of these fields. No clear
landmarks are visible here making navigation a challenge. Seasons of fallen
fruit can sustain the wildlife population here, as burrowed holes are evidence.
name: Somewhere in the Grass

desc:
Short green sod clump between tall lanky patches of wild oats and bent grass,
forming an interesting texture on this fairway. A great forest grows far to the
east. A deep gulch crosses the landscape to the south preventing travel in that
direction, while the plains continue to the west.
name: Wide Fields of Grain

desc:
Wide stretches of wild grasses are shaded yellow by the abundance of mustard
growing here. The gentle rolling countryside sweeps easily in almost all
directions. The peacefulness of this gentle pasture contrasts with the dangerous
sharp drop to the south.
name: Fields of Yellow

desc:
Expansive fields of yellow and deep blue iris join the endless grasses of the
plains. A stand of barren trees and bushes grows tightly bound together by
invasive flowering vines, and provides some comfort from the elements. These
vines are blanketed with sprays of delicate pale pink buds which contribute a
sweet, intoxicating perfume throughout these plains.
name: Broad Plains

desc:
Green sweeps of maidengrass, oat and ryegrasses flourish in this lush valley.
Wildflowers and small bushes are common here. With all the burrow holes found
around these rich pastures, and the abundance of grains, it seems this area
would be suitable to many species of small animals.
name: Central Plains

desc:
The plains continue as a full circle of every shade of green. This is a mixed
field of alfalfa and ryegrasses. The skyline in all directions is only slightly
disturbed by an occasional young willow tree wading through the grasses.
name: Airy Plains

desc:
The grassy landscape gently tumbles downward west, towards the distant Anduin.
This area is known for the fragrant edelweiss that grows in fringed white mounds
throughout the year. Small patches of pebble-hewn earth are exposed in places
although wildflowers and low shrubbery thrive in this soil. The plains' meadows
and fields continue in all directions.
name: Grassy Sweep

desc:
A shallow gully widens to the west, scarring the border of the green meadow. On
either side, a couple of old scrub oaks glare at each other's gnarled figures.
These fields are full of new grasses that clump together and form soft green
islands between the mature shrubbery. The plains continue in every direction.
name: A Shallow Gully

desc:
Huge, naturalized beds of creeping lily turf stand out in the ocean of oat and
ryegrasses. The lush landscape includes an abundance of small shrubs and varied
weeds. Gusty breezes that play upon these emerald fields can cause mesmerizing
ripples. To the east a large rock formation can be noticed above the horizon.
name: Lilies in the Grass

desc:
Wildflowers, grasses and shrubs compete with the many different weeds for space
in these broad meadows. During the warmer months, the light fragrance of sweet
grasses lingers throughout these expansive plains. To the far west, a dark line
of blue marks the course of the river Anduin; beyond that, the Misty Mountains
begin their ascent into the clouds.
name: Wildflowers and Grasses

desc:
Dark towering oak trees grow together making a complete canopy which stretches
upwards into the darkness. Large amounts of forest litter make ample food for
the vast array of large fleshy fungi that exist here. The forest extends to the
north and west, and opens to the south to a field of wildflowers and east to a
vast array of ferns and dense vegetation.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Large dark trunks grow around a rocky outlet here. The soil has been upturned
recently which may mean that something large frequents this area. A large cave
can be seen through fallen logs and undergrowth. Mosses grow over the rocks here
and the sound of dripping water can be heard nearby. The dark forest continues
in all directions.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The tall trees that grow here are interspersed with smaller trees and brush
which makes travel here slow. Some of the large trees have large claw marks down
their trunks. Sap runs down from these injuries, creating sticky lines which
seem to attract insects. Through some of the brush small game trails can be
seen, marked by a large variation of tracks. The forest continues in all
directions.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
The massive oak trees grow so tall they disappear into a gloomy darkness. No
sounds can be heard that are typical of forest creatures, as it seems they have
all deserted, or been eaten. Huge strands of straggly moss hang here from the
lower rotten branches of these trees, giving this area a very menacing
appearance. Small mushrooms appear between the upturned roots of these oaks,
making use of the nutrients held within the forest floor.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
This area of the forest is composed of very large trees that grow so close
together as to blot out the entire sky. The forest floor is composed of the old
leaves from oaks and beeches that grow here. Very little grows in this forest
humus, except for the occasional mushroom. To the east the forest quickly opens
to a grass field. To the north, south, and west the forest continues.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Thick rich forest humus meets grassy field east of here as the forest becomes
less dense and opens up. Bushes and small shrubs struggle with larger trees for
dominance. There is a trail north of here and more of the forest continues to
the south. The forest to the west becomes darker.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The trail continues east and west, with dark forest looming in from the north
and south. Little foot-travel is evidenced here by the tufts of grass growing
between the small rocks and dirt that make up this trail. Further to the east
the trail seems to cut through a large grassy meadow, and even farther beyond
plunges into an even larger and darker forest. Far off to the west, the Misty
Mountains can barely be discerned.
name: Trail through a Forest

desc:
Light breaks through this part of the canopy faintly as the forest meets the
eastern grass fields. There are small bushes that make use of any limited
sunlight through the forest canopy, but cannot exist further east because of the
dense grasses that grow there. The forest becomes darker the further west one
travels. A small trail can be seen south of here.
name: In a Dense Forest

desc:
The tree density is so great here that the canopies of the trees cover all the
sky above it. A thick layer of humus covers the forest ground because of the
constant decay of fallen leaves and dead plants. Some toadstools thrive on the
rich soil. To the south is too much vegetation, which makes passage impossible.
The vegetation in the south is mostly thorny bushes and brambles.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The ground is covered by some dirt and small pebbles which make up the trail.
Some grass blades push up between the pebbles and dirt of the trail. Beside the
trail grow some oak trees, that make up the border of the forest to the south
and north. There is dense shrubbery to the north making movement there
impossible. To the east and west the trail continues onwards through the forest.
name: Small Trail through a Forest

desc:
Mostly oak trees grow here, with a few mosses thriving on the rich soil of humus
that lies on the ground. Many small twigs and broken off branches lie on the
ground, which would create a cracking sound when stepped on. To the west and
north the trail can be seen lying in the forest. In the south and east the view
into the forest is made difficult because of the dense growth of the trees.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Different kinds of toadstools grow on the oak trees, thriving in the dark. Also
some moss grows on the damp forest floor. All around one can not see far into
the forest because of the many trees blocking the view.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Due to the densely packed branches and leaves of the trees, the canopy blocks
out almost all sunlight, making it almost totally dark. On the ground grow some
dark species of toadstools almost integrated with their surroundings. One can't
see far in the thick and dense forest.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The darkness here is created due to the dense packing of leaves in the canopy of
the trees. Fungus and some mushrooms grow on the thick layer of humus that
covers the ground of the forest.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
Mosses and fungi thrive on the forest soil in the inky darkness. The trees have
almost no branches growing from ground level until halfway up. Due to the dense
packaging of trees around, one can not see very far into the forest around.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
On the ground grows almost nothing save a few mosses. The canopy above is so
dense that it blocks all view of the sky above and sunlight during day. All
around one can only see a short distance into the dark forest, because of the
many trees hindering view.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
In between the roots of the trees grow some toadstools and an occasional fungus.
On the bark of most of the trees grows some moss. The branches of the trees near
ground level have almost no leaves on them due to lack of sunlight.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Young trees flourish here as the forest comes to an end. Moss grows over most of
the landscape, making the footing very soft. Plains surround the forest on all
sides except to the north, where the forest grows thicker and the gloom of
Mirkwood lies heavier.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
Here the grass grows two feet tall in most places. The thick, knotted roots make
it difficult to walk here, but not impossible. A forest can be seen to the
north, and a deep gully hardens the travel to the south.
name: Near a Forest

desc:
A deep gully cuts through the grassy meadows to the north and south. Some
flowers grow on the southern side of the gully, while many plants thrive on the
northern side, emitting a strong, sweet fragrance as they sway in the soft
breeze. Returning eastward up the gully is impossible due to the cliffs in that
direction. Descending westward is possible, with skilled climbers able to do so
safely. Alternatively, exits to the north or south are available, as well as the
option to ascend the rocky banks in either direction.
name: A Deep Gully

desc:
The footpath wriggles its way through the high grass, without getting too close
to the deep gully in the north, continuing to the west and south. A stone lies
close to the edge, perhaps placed there to point out where the gully is. A few
trees are seen to the south-west.
name: Footpath Overlooking a Gully

desc:
The narrow path goes north and south. It borders a small orchard located to the
west. Not many feet seem to have trodden this path recently, and yet it is not
overgrown: someone has been using it. A few trees are seen in the western
direction and behind them the outline of a building can be noticed a bit further
northwards. The plains seem to end abruptly far to the north but they continue
eastwards.
name: Footpath

desc:
The path leads north and south, passing by a lush meadow. It is not overgrown,
yet it doesn't seem to be used too much. On the border of the path there are
clumps of higher and taller grass, as though the soil in these spots were
enriched with something.
name: Footpath

desc:
The track goes north and south, passing a lonely apple tree growing on the west
side. The path does not look very trodden; patches of grass are starting to
invade it from both sides. Nevertheless, it is not completely overgrown but will
soon be, if not used by animals or other travellers.
name: Footpath

desc:
The track bends to the north and west. On its southern side it is bordered by
tall and thick grass, tall enough to hide a deer or a man. There are not many
footprints left on the path. Brave adventurers seeking Mirkwood, and animals
that simply do not want to perform strenuous travelling in the foliage, are
probable causes why these types of paths remain beaten despite the grassy
environment.
name: Bending Footpath

desc:
Yellowish in colour, this narrow track leads west-east among the grass. To the
south, dense foliage obscures the view, and to the north, a few trees can be
spotted. The grass has grown tall enough to hide even the least careful of small
animals, and occasional animal sounds verify the existence of wild life
underneath.
name: Footpath

desc:
Planted here many years ago, this apple tree looks strangely out of place.
Someone must have taken care of it, since the boughs have been purposely
trimmed. There are no other fruit trees around, nor orchards, and if it were not
for a path visible to the east and south, there would be no other sign of
people.
name: Old Apple Tree

desc:
Located between a pond in the west and a path to the east, this meadow does not
differ much from many other meadows that can be found in the Vale of Anduin.
Green grass studded with white, red, blue and yellow flowers covers the area. A
lonely tree may be spotted in the south.
name: Wet Meadow

desc:
This is an orchard with a few apple trees, a few pear trees, and some plum
trees. It is not big nor in a very good condition, but it fulfills its role. The
orchard is bordered by a small footpath to the north and east, and by meadows
south and westward. In the southwestern direction a silvery pond can be seen.
name: Orchard

desc:
The path leads west and east through different varieties of foliage, passing
numerous colourful plants and bushes on its way. The ground descends in a slope
continuing towards a gully to the north. What look like apple-trees can be seen
a bit further south.
name: Footpath

desc:
The gully abruptly breaks the monotony of the fields surrounding it. Red flowers
and small plants of different shades of yellow cover the northern parts, while
pale green grass and blue flowers grow to the south. The gully widens to the
east, but the slope is too steep to be reached from this direction.
name: A Shallow Gully

desc:
The plain is covered with grass rising nearly one foot above ground, and not
many other plants have managed to grow taller. Occasional bushes and stones
break the monotony of the shifting grass. Very little foliage grows in the shade
to the north, but it grows more densely in the other directions. A gully to the
south makes the journey southwards somewhat hard but not impossible.
name: Tall Grass by the Edge of a Gully

desc:
The plains here are surrounded by forest in every direction but to the south.
Shadows cover the plains most of the day. The grass is suffering from lack of
sunlight and is dying in most places. The forest will most likely take over this
area soon.
name: A Shadowed Plain

desc:
The forest floor is covered with a thin layer of humus in which some small
bushes and shrubs grow. To the east and north the forest gets a lot darker, in
the far west one can see grasslands.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
The forest is all dark because of the thick canopy above blocking out all light.
Some fungus grows on the ground, among the entangled roots of the trees around.
To the south the forest becomes slightly less dense.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The soil in the forest is made up of very dark organic material, originating
from decayed branches and dead shrubs. Some small dark shrubs grow on the
ground, where some mosses also grow. To the west the forest become less densely
packed with trees.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
Due to the dense growth of trees, its canopy makes it all dark. Some small
toadstools and fungus grow in the inky blackness on the rich dark soil. To the
west one can barely see a plain stretching out very far into the west.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
Because of the dense growth of trees, the ground is totally covered by the
darkness. On the ground fallen leaves and dead shrubs are decaying, forming the
nourishment for the tall trees. To the north the trail can be seen bending
northwards.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The trail bends to the north and west here. In the north the trail enters the
forest west of Mirkwood. The trail is covered with dirt and gravel and is
slightly overgrown by small plants and grass due to little use. In the east and
south is a forest densely packed with oak and other kinds of trees.
name: At a Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
The path in the forest is covered with a thin layer of rotten leaves and freshly
fallen ones. To the north one can barely spot a path in the woods which has
fallen into disuse and begun to grow over. In the south one can see the trail
bend westwards, while in the east the trail continues on through the forest.
name: Trail through the Border of a Forest

desc:
Some small shrubs grow here, below the canopy of trees, where the rays of the
sun shine during the day. To the south a barely visible path can be seen through
the dense shrubs that grow there. To the west stretch grasslands with a dip in
them to the far west.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
There are some flowerbeds with various kinds of colour scattered around in the
grasslands. In the west the grasslands suddenly fall into a depression because
of a dip in the plains. To the north and east the border of a forest can be seen
and in the south is a small dark cave, that lies in a hill to the south.
name: Border of a Forest

desc:
There are a lot of trees growing in the grass filled plains, bordering the
forest to the east. To the northwest the treetops of the trees are lower than
all around because of a dip in the plains there, to the south runs a trail
through the grasslands and in the north is a wall of rocks standing against a
hill.
name: Near a Bend in the Trail

desc:
Bare soil makes up the most part of the trail in the west, with a few pebbles in
it. To the east the trail becomes more made out of gravel with small stones. The
grass growing beside the road has a healthy green colour, making a juicy treat
for herbivores. To the far north a small hill can be spotted.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
There is some moss growing on the ground. Some small bushes grow below the
trees, just there where they can catch a few rays of sunlight during day. The
trees are somewhat apart from each other, letting the sun shine through the
canopy during day. To the north is the trail that runs through the forest,
heading into Mirkwood.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
In between the trees grow some shrubs and bushes barely able to catch a few
sunrays. Through the canopy of the trees one can see the sky above. To the west
a small pond can be seen in the outstretching plains. To the north the vague
lining of a trail can be seen through the forest, to the south is a small
meadow.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
Amongst the small flowers like dandelion and passion flower grow small bushes in
the meadow. The grass here has a healthy dark green colour. Also there grow some
trees in the meadow, probably sprouted from seed from the forest around. In the
west a plain can be seen spreading itself out, further around only forest can be
seen almost enclosing the whole meadow.
name: Small Meadow beside a Forest

desc:
Small plants and bushes grow below the trees catching some sunrays that filter
through the canopy above during daytime. To the west grows some very high grass
with some trees in it, to the south and east the forest gets thicker packed with
trees.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
Small bushes and shrubs grow below the canopy of the trees. The tall trees that
grow here have some mosses growing on them. To the west one can see the plain
with very high grasses stretching out to the horizon, to the east the forest
becomes dark.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
During daytime some sunlight filters through the canopy of the trees,
illuminating the forest floor. In between the trees grow some plants and bushes.
To the northeast the forest gets dark, to the west the grasslands spread out in
all directions to the horizon.
name: In a Light Forest

desc:
The forest is very light in this area. In most places, where the forest gives
way to the plains, the trees are spread apart from each other by at least ten
feet. Travelling through here is, however, not very easy due to the large amount
of vines that hang from tree to tree, and the slippery mosses and fungi
underfoot.
name: At the Edge of a Forest

desc:
High grass and small plants grow on the plain, swaying softly in the wind. Few
flowers grow here, and thick roots make it somewhat hard to traverse this plain.
Close to the north, in the edge of a light forest, trees grow sparsely. Further
northeast, however, the forest has grown denser and nothing is discernible,
except the menacing trees. The grassy plains continue in every other direction
and the foliage seems to have grown taller than usual to the west.
name: Near a Forest

desc:
The grass colours the plain in different shades of green as the never-ending
wind shifts it softly. A few very large stones lie scattered about on the plain
as if giants have been tossing them in random directions. A house rises to the
southwest, and a gully leads down in the eastern direction, splitting the
grasslands. The outskirts of a forest lie not too far to the north.
name: Plains by a Gully

desc:
A small path can be discerned here, leading off to the east in a fairly straight
line. It is lined by tall green grass and a few stones, and it has likely been
tended by someone to prevent the grass on each side to merge.
name: End of a Footpath

desc:
A patch of grassland has been carved out from the wilderness and turned for
man's use. It is a pasture, and a good one for that. It lies just north of a
little pond, so that the animals can have access to the water without having to
cross large fields. The grass is lush and the plants seem very healthy. A line
of trees border it on the east side. To the northwest stands a wooden building.
name: Pasture

desc:
Among the grass, a placid pond reflects the sky. The water seems safe to drink,
and indeed, tracks of domestic and wild animals prove that it is used for this
purpose. In the reeds on the shore insects buzz busily. Green meadows lie all
around, covered with grass.
name: Pond

desc:
The terrain slopes a little, descending delicately to the pond in the north. The
ground is wet, but not so much as to flood the plants. In fact, it is just
perfect for water-thyme and other water-loving plants as well as animals.
name: Wet Meadow

desc:
A footpath dissolves among the grass and the plants. It is small and overgrown
in places, without many footprints evident. One barely discernible branch bends
to the south, leading into a small clump of willows. The path is more pronounced
to the east, whereas it leaves no trace to the west.
name: Waning Footpath

desc:
Here the plains seem particularly lively. The grass reflects a deep green
colour, unlike the dusty green that is usual on the steppes. The foliage has
grown tall enough to reach the knees of a man, and small animals are likely to
use it is as shelter. The plains continue in all directions. A patch of flowers
can be seen to the west giving the grassland a slightly different nuance.
name: Lush Grass

desc:
The ground appears to slope a little here, and is soaked with water. Different
kinds of plants thrive here, and all kinds of mud-loving animals. Even though
the place looks very much like a mire, it is firm enough to support the weight
of any animal that may pass by.
name: Wet Meadow

desc:
The ground is soaked with water, forming small, shallow puddles here and there.
The grass grows unhindered, except for the animals grazing on its sweetness.
Meadows spread south, west and north. A whiff of moisture from the east betrays
the presence of a small pond there.
name: Wet Meadow

desc:
This meadow is covered with low, green grass. Many small paths cross it in all
directions, with evident traces of wild life. The northern side is blocked by
the wall the of a building, but all other directions are open and covered with
