more grass.
name: Meadow

desc:
The grass rises many inches above the ground and is of a yellow colour as well
as many shades of green. Many small stones lie gathered in a pile in the middle
of the plain and flowers have been placed on and around it. Ivy grows high along
the house wall to the south, almost hiding it. The ground rises slightly to the
west.
name: Tall Grass North of a House

desc:
High grass makes the journey through the plains slightly strenuous to
accomplish. The grass has grown really tall, as far as the eye can see. A few
flowers have managed to grow higher than the grass - those who have, stand
proudly defying the wind while the grass shifts softly. A cluster of trees
stands to the north-east and a long hedge has been planted to the west. The
faint outline of a roof with a chimney can be glimpsed beyond the high grass,
straight to the south.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The grass grows high in this area, nearly to the armpits of an average man. To
the east, the trees being to be take the plains over. Some holly bushes and wild
flowers grow in patches where the grass isn't quite so high, taking away some of
the monotony of the otherwise drab plains.
name: Tall Grass beside a Forest

desc:
There are more trees growing in the grass nearer to the light forest in the
east. Also some big shrubs grow in between the medium high grass. To the north
the grass gets longer, while it diminishes in length in the south. In the east
is a light forest and further east the forest becomes dark.
name: Medium High Grass near a Forest

desc:
The grassland beside the forest in the east is full of high grass with some
lonely trees growing in it. A few plants are also growing here, which have
succeeded to grow here. To the south the grass becomes shorter.
name: Grassland besides a Forest

desc:
The grass growing here is very long, making it difficult for other kinds of
vegetation to grow here. A handful of trees have succeeded to sprout here,
making a change in scenery besides grass. To the east one can see the rim of a
light forest.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass that grows here seems to form a sea of grass. In between the grass
grows some trees. Small paths are going in every direction here made by animals,
which have trampled the grass when passing through here, foraging for food and
water. To the north and east the grass becomes shorter, whereas it becomes
longer in the west and south.
name: Grass Sea

desc:
The plain is mostly covered with grass, with some flowers and trees in it. In
the middle of the plain is a small pond with reeds growing at its bank. To the
east is the rim of a forest, to the north one can spot a small trail in the
plain.
name: At a Small Pond in the Grasslands

desc:
The grass becomes taller here, making travel through it somewhat difficult. Some
small trees are growing in between the grass with their branches waving softly
in the wind. To the north and west is a trail, in the east the border of a
forest can be seen.
name: Medium Grass near a Trail

desc:
The trail here is mostly bare soil with only a few pebbles embedded into it. In
the west the trail is more covered with pebbles and gravel. Beside the trail
grows mostly grass and an occasional tree, casting their shadow during day over
the plains.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
There are moss covered rocks scattered all around these grasslands. To the
north, the plains slope into a small depression and to the south, a minor trail
runs both east and west. A large forest is in the east, but the plains continue
to stretch to the horizon in the west. To the northwest is a small hill.
name: Rocky Grasslands

desc:
The plains here slope downward into a depression and are covered by numerous
shrubs and trees. All around is elevated plains, in the east one can barely see
the canopy of a forest. To the southeast is a small hill.
name: Dip in the Grasslands

desc:
There are some solitary trees growing on the plains, also some flowers grow here
and there. To the east a dip in the plains can be seen, in the west is a small
forest. A small trail runs through the vast plains in the south leading into the
west and east.
name: Grasslands beside a Forest

desc:
The grass that is growing here is all over an inch high, some small weeds and
mosses grow in the grass. To the northwest a small forest can be seen which is a
change in scenery in the ongoing plains. To the south a small trail runs through
the grasslands, entering a forest at the east side of the trail. In the
northeast the plains fall into a local depression of the plains.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Through the grasslands runs a minor trail bending in the south to the west and
continuing on towards the east. The trail is covered by some gravel. The
grassland surrounding the trail is full of trees and small bushes.
name: Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
Small pebbles and gravel make up the most part of the trail, the remainder of it
is bare rock. Here and there the trail is somewhat heightened by the roots of
the trees growing beside the trail. Beside the trail the grasslands stretch out
to the east and south, and to the north one can see the trail bending east.
name: Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
The grass is about twenty inches tall making moving through it pretty difficult.
There are some solitary trees growing in the sea of grass. To the north is a
minor trail bending eastwards in the far north.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
The grass here is extremely high, with a few trees in between it, surrounded by
a seemingly endless sea of grass. Here and there are paths going criss- cross
through the grass made by animals searching for food. To the north the grass
becomes shorter, further north one can see a small trail.
name: High Grass

desc:
The trees growing in the high grass are sticking out above the grass only for
three-quarter of their length. There are only a few plants which grow in between
the grass. All around one can only see high grass save a few plants and trees.
name: High Grass

desc:
The trees that grow here solemnly in between the high grass is the only other
sort of vegetation that grows here. To the south the height of the grass
diminishes to a medium length.
name: High Grass

desc:
The grass here is as high as a rabbit standing upright. In the grass grow a few
trees, casting their shadow over the grass during daytime. To the north one can
see the grass become longer with only a few trees in it and in the east one can
barely see the border of a forest.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
The grasslands on this even ground here have grown strong here, the grass being
of a lustrous green colour. A few dandelions and other yellow flowers grow in
small groups a bit to the west, looking very healthy as they have grown taller
than the grass. Trees are seen towering towards the sky to the east. The
grasslands continue in all directions and a long hedge stands to the south and
south-west.
name: Grassland

desc:
The shrubs with red and white blossoms are covered with small berries and rise
about three feet above the ground. The bushes are planted in a long row, leading
off to the west and forming a hedge. Yellow grass surrounding the shrubs has
grown taller than on the other parts of the plain, which likely means that
someone has watered the hedge. The ground rises gently to the south, forming a
slope, and the grasslands continue to the north and east. A light forest can be
seen in a few miles away to the northeast.
name: Hawthorns

desc:
The slope leads downwards to the north. The many colours of different grasses
and flowers shift in the blowing wind. The whining of the wind brings an
occasional sound of animal activity as it passes through the plains. A long
green hedge can be spotted in the western direction leading north. A wooden
cabin has been built to the south-east. A tidily-grown row of bushes blocks the
way to the south.
name: Gentle Slope

desc:
Flat and grassy, this stretch of the ground shows signs of use. Here and there a
small circle has been grazed out in the grass. In other places, some low ditches
and barriers have been built out of pressed earth. A house stands to the
northeast and a row of bushes has been planted to block the way north.
name: Grassland

desc:
This vegetation could be the dream for any animal that eats grass - tall, green,
and juicy. A few flowers grow in a cluster but many more seem to have been eaten
and only the stem remains. Apparently there is a water source not very far to
the east since the soil looks even more wet in that direction. Tracks of all
kinds confirm the presence of wild life.
name: Lush Grass

desc:
The ground seems a little wet, and the flowers use this moisture to the outmost.
The grass is very thick, green and juicy. Herbivores would likely satisfy their
hunger on these fields and indeed tracks criss-crossing the fields show sign of
wild life.
name: Lush Grass

desc:
Here the plains are covered with flowers - bluebonnets and poppies. The climate
allows them to bloom during most of the year, creating a colourful carpet on the
face of the steppe. Plants here are thicker and greener than their cousins to
the south and west, probably because they have more moisture and nutrition. The
fields to the north and east seem to sport vegetation just as healthy. A few
stones can be seen, around which different plants have taken root.
name: Blue and Red Flowers

desc:
A simple trellised gate, constructed solely of white peeling-birch branches,
secures the grounds inside. The gate handle looks to be fashioned from an old
metal buckle. Glossy green vines with small white berries encircle the sturdy
gateposts, intertwine through the trellis overhead, and trail down on both sides
of the entrance. There is a hook to hang a lantern from affixed to the post. A
worn path leads east through this small gateway.
name: Birch Gate

desc:
A large mound of dirt in the centre of this small earthen cave is filled with
debris fallen from above. Leaves, grasses and other refuse mixed with the clay
soil makes for unstable footing. Roots of all sizes protrude from the walls in a
stringy, tangly mass.
name: Dirty Hollow

desc:
The bottom of the crevice is filled with rocks and thorny vines that seem to
claim the southern side. This is the most impossible area of the ravine. Deeply
set sides of the canyon and crumbling earth prevent an easy climb back to the
crest of the gully.
name: Darkbottom Ravine

desc:
Bright leaves fringe young saplings above the flowered meadow. Azaleas bloom in
reds and pinks surrounded by ferns of all textures. The ground is a rich deep
brown broken by mounds of soft mosses. A tight stand of trees blocks entry to
the south.
name: Meadow Greenery

desc:
The walls of the cave are smooth and damp. Some water drips from a large
stalactite that hangs from the ceiling. Below the stalactite a small pool of
water has formed. Bones of different size are lying in one corner of the cave,
left after the owners of the bones had been eaten.
name: In a Bear's Den

desc:
Above the land, this immense boulder offers views for miles in all directions.
At the western side, an ancient fir tree has rooted firmly in a long jagged
crack, offering shade on hot clear days. On the eastern end, the rock juts up
and a little spring of clear fresh water collects in a smooth weather-worn bowl
of blue granite before it streams down the face of this great stone.
name: Upon a Mossy Rock

desc:
Perched atop this rocky peak, there is a clear view of the surrounding area. The
natural formation of this jutting pinnacle is just roomy enough to allow a
comfortable spot to rest and a broad stone ledge to lean upon here above the
treetops.
name: Stone Pinnacle

desc:
The rank stench of rotting pith can jar all the senses in these subterranean
confines. Old cobwebs hang from dirt-clotted roots above, seeming to blow back
and forth in the stark stillness of this reeking chamber.
name: Humid Cavern

desc:
Boneshards litter the outer perimeter of this horrid space. The earthen walls
are draped in layers of webs which seem to contain bundles swathed in more silk.
The humidity is unbearable here and noxiously foul odours are only too evident.
name: The Lair of the Spider

desc:
This shack is constructed of logs from this forest. Mud between the timbers
insulates the hut nicely. Broad beams cross the pitched roof allowing room for
little ropes of drying herbs and meats. Small windows on each wall have been
fitted with twig shutters. There is a simple cot in the corner.
name: Home of a Woodman

desc:
The grassy courtyard is surrounded by the lumberman's work-tables. To the south
firewood is piled uniformly under a rough flat timber. An area solely dedicated
to preparation of animal skins and hides is just inside the gate. A rustic
lean-to near the woodpile protects the domestic animals. Tools hang on hooks
mounted on the side of the animal shelter. There is a gate to the west.
name: Country Courtyard

desc:
The ground in front of the woodman's hut is covered in river stones. The smooth
rocks have been arranged in many circles like the age-rings of a tree. To the
north you can see the rustic hut and to the east a field. Along the western side
a broad hedge grows tall. To the south there is a courtyard.
name: River Stone Circles

desc:
This small robust hut is constructed out of logs of rough timber. There is a
crude stove and above it hang some pots and pans made of thick iron. A simple
board table sits in the middle of the room flanked by sturdy wooden benches. A
small bed covered with furs sits in the corner across the room. A window looks
out through the eastern wall.
name: Inside a Woodman's Hut

desc:
This is a very well-tended wheat field and its harvest grows lush in this rich
soil. The field is divided by shallow ditches designed to prevent the field from
flooding and also to properly irrigate the soil.
name: Golden Wheat Fields

desc:
This is a very well-tended garden with a fine variety of flowers and bushes. The
garden is divided into several sections. Areas with fruit trees, vegetables and
a shaded spot for mushrooms can be found here. There is a portion dedicated to
herbal plants.
name: Lush Garden

desc:
Mouldy odours of dank moist soil fill this darkened grotto. The footing here is
slippery black mud with lumpy clots of dirt. Long tree roots drape like thick
netting from the cavern's ceiling and re-enter the wet soil below.
name: Exposed Roots

desc:
A cramped burrow adds needed storage space for the hut above. Simple beechwood
shelves line the compacted dirt walls in this tiny root cellar. The cool dark
confines are right for storing cool weather vegetables and other foodstuffs.
name: Crawl Space

desc:
Scattered among the bones and debris are piles of dung and old carcasses. A foul
odour of decay and filth pervades this area. It is the stink of trolls. Upheaved
stumps play the role of stools and table, being covered in blood and entrails.
name: A Dark Burrow

desc:
Large trees here take on gruesome shapes as they entwine together with their
boughs and branches. Strings of grey moss entangle the branches, which only adds
to their strange appearance. To the east the dark forest continues, while in the
west, south, and north the forest becomes even darker.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The forest grows in an orderly fashion here. The dark trees here look as if
someone planted them long ago. However, there is also a lack of decay on these
trees. There are no signs of rot here, and no trees are fallen over. The forest
floor is devoid of typical life such as mushrooms. The canopy overhead is a mass
of huge branches intertwining together to choke out the sky. No sound can be
heard in this area, but a pale light can be seen dancing near to the north.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Huge black trees grow here almost in a certain chaotic arrangement. Twisted
boughs intertwine with one another to block out the sun and sky. The weather is
oppressive here, as no breeze can reach this part of the forest. Silence hangs
here, as no forest creature settle in these branches. A pale unnatural light can
be seen to the east.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
A pale eldritch light shines in this area, its source unknown. The trees which
surround this area form a circle here. The huge boughs form a canopy so tight
nothing of the sky can be seen. The silence which has been so prevalent in the
nearby areas is broken here by soft murmuring from under the forest floor. Some
large stones mark this area. There are also some large footprints, which means
some immense creature lives here.
name: Glade of Darkness

desc:
This room seems composed of roots and dirt specifically shaped and molded to fit
whomever designed it. The roots have even been shaped to a throne of sorts. The
ceiling is composed of roots that have formed the beams of this room. If its
creators had intended to mock the dignity of a temple, they have succeeded.
name: A Dark Forest Chapel

desc:
This is the room in which Eblees presumably sleeps. The roots and tendrils have
shaped the room according to the forest priest's wishes. A bed of tendrils and
humus lays at one side of the room while a table of roots with strangely shaped
chairs sits at another. A stone chest has been set against the foot of the bed.
name: The Cloister

desc:
The forest here looks ordered, in its darkness. There is only the faintest smell
of rot here, and there is a lack of bones and other such forest litter which
seems common in the other parts of this forest. The canopy seems to be comprised
of only large branches that hang far up overhead.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
Thick strange looking trees of all shapes twist upwards to the darkness of the
canopy overhead. One large tree grows here that stands straight and tall in
sharp contrast to the chaotic forest that surrounds it. This one tree is also
remarkable because it has a much wider base than the other trees here. Gloomy
darkness is all that can be seen here.
name: Dark Trees

desc:
The air here is stifling as so many thick branches close in around this tree.
Many branches twist and tangle making a tunnel going up and down. Looking down
there is darkness of the forest, a musty foetid odour of the forest floor reeks
in this direction. In the treetop above, some skylight can be seen.
name: Within the Forest Canopy

desc:
The forest canopy of Mirkwood opens up into the sky here. It is a sharp contrast
of sky and open air, compared to the darkness and shadowed canopy below. Birds,
butterflies and clouds can be seen floating in the sky overhead. A breeze
catches the other treetops and creates waves with the leaves as if this were
only a meadow.
name: The Top of a Large Tree

desc:
Quickly dug, this hole is much deeper than one can expect from above. The walls
below the edge have been carved in, to make the base wider than the entrance.
Piles of dirt and rocks have caved in and lie scattered across the uneven floor.
Twigs and branches have tumbled down from above, as someone or something crashed
through the foliage that once covered this hole and created a blocking layer of
dense debris that almost block out all light.
name: A Deep Hole

desc:
This secluded clearing contains knee-high dry grass. In all directions forest
can be seen. To the north the trees grow most densely, making travel impossible
in that direction.
name: Hollow in the Forest

desc:
Fuzzy green moss grows up the northern facing tree trunks, favouring only that
side of the trees. The deep forest gloom seems to grow darker and more dismal in
every direction. South the trees grow substantially denser preventing any
movement in that direction. Small shafts of light dapple down through the
treetops, imprinting faint patterns in the blackness of the forest floor.
name: Dappled Darkness

desc:
The tall and big trees growing here are almost all covered by toadstools. In the
darkness one should watch their step, because the roots of the trees are lying
everywhere. The dark forest continues to the south and east, while to the west
and northwest the forest gets less dark.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
Thick trees grow together here, blocking out all skylight. Nothing else grows
here except strange looking fungi. Some of the trees are covered with a thick
slimy substance. There is a large fallen tree that can be seen in the darkness.
Moss has grown over most of this log, and the smell of decaying wood is
prevalent, especially around this log.
name: In a Dark Forest

desc:
The forest humus above has given way to some dark force which has created this
slimy covered hole. The smell of rotting flesh pervades this hole and piles of
bone mixed with pieces of flesh rest here in slimy ooze. Something large has
existed here for a long time, making a meal of anything which comes in from
above. It does not seem to be a wise place to tarry.
name: A Slimy Dark Hole
